result.summary: Mapped ClickHouse’s public developer surfaces on clickhouse.com: 1 ClickHouse Cloud HTTP API, 1 CLI (clickhousectl), and 2 MCP endpoints (Cloud Remote MCP and ClickStack MCP), authenticated via Cloud API keys and/or Cloud OAuth depending on surface.

clickhouse_cloud_api_key.label: ClickHouse Cloud API key ID and secret
clickhouse_cloud_api_key.setup: In the ClickHouse Cloud console, create an API key under the Cloud API key management documented at [Managing API keys](https://clickhouse.com/docs/cloud/manage/openapi). The OpenAPI section includes endpoints to list/create keys, and the Cloud CLI docs say you can log in with an API key and secret. Use the issued key ID as the Basic auth username and the secret as the Basic auth password.

clickhouse_cloud_oauth.label: ClickHouse Cloud account OAuth login
clickhouse_cloud_oauth.setup: For Cloud surfaces that support interactive login, start from the product flow rather than registering an app: either connect your MCP client to the ClickHouse Cloud MCP endpoint and complete the OAuth 2.0 browser flow described at [Remote MCP in Cloud](https://clickhouse.com/docs/cloud/features/ai-ml/remote-mcp), or run `clickhousectl cloud auth login` as described in the ClickHouse CLI docs/blog. This grants the CLI or MCP client access to your ClickHouse Cloud account.
